steps.title: Use the sample-size rule
steps.content: The passage says a representative handedness study needs at least 176 individuals. The meta-analysis does not say captive samples are automatically representative; it gives a numerical minimum for any handedness study. Merrick's and Porter's studies each included only 8 individuals, far below that threshold.

steps.title: Apply the same standard
steps.content: B and D wrongly treat Merrick's small study as reliable. C speculates about underestimation, but the passage only supports a sample-size problem. Both studies fail the same standard equally, so both are too small for a meaningful comparison. The answer is A.
